title: L003: Add operator resilience and workload chaos tests
summary: Post-external-review improvement cycle addressing 3 findings from berendt. W-001: Job cleanup for re-runnability added to SC-CHAOS-005 Step 4 via kubectl delete --ignore-not-found before create. W-002: Kill-detection assertion added to SC-CHAOS-008 Step 5 to prevent vacuous passes when PodChaos never takes effect. I-001: Echo message changed from 'PDB enforced' to 'Availability maintained during pod kill' to accurately describe runtime validation scope. Review pattern files updated with correct occurrence counts and new CC-0048 examples. All fixes are minimal, correct, and introduce no new issues.

tests_checklist.item: W-001 fix: Job cleanup before creation makes SC-CHAOS-005 Step 4 idempotent and re-runnable
W-002 fix: Kill-detection assertion prevents vacuous pass when PodChaos selector mismatch or Chaos Mesh not running
I-001 fix: Echo message accurately describes runtime availability validation, not PDB enforcement
Fixes follow existing Chainsaw test patterns (numbered steps, catch blocks, diagnostic scripts)
No new test logic gaps introduced by the changes

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing chaos E2E test suite structure pattern
Changes are minimal — only addresses external review findings
YAML indentation and structure correct after multiline script change
Defensive defaults (${READY:-0}) used consistently in kill-detection assertion

security_checklist.item: No secrets or credentials in test files
No command injection vectors in kubectl commands

architecture_checklist.item: No scope creep — only files flagged by external review are modified
Review pattern files correctly updated with new occurrence counts

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No unnecessary code added beyond the fixes

fail_fast_checklist.item: Kill-detection assertion fails fast with descriptive error before availability check
Job deletion uses --ignore-not-found for safe idempotency

defensive_checklist.item: Pre-creation cleanup guards against stale Job leftovers from previous runs
Explicit loop-exhaustion check prevents silent vacuous passes

suggested_improvements: Minor: tests/e2e-chaos/api-pod-kill-pdb/chainsaw-test.yaml:92 — inline comment still says '(PDB enforcement)' while the echo on line 98 was updated to 'Availability maintained during pod kill' per I-001. Consider aligning the comment for consistency, though this is not a functional issue.
